campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth with recreation scholarships. They also discussed plans to improve bicycle and pedestrian safety near the Hamilton Avenue overpass by Highway 17, including approving a feasibility study and applying for grant funding to help pay for the project.
keyTopics: $4,000 youth scholarship donation from Water Tower Kitchen
Hamilton Avenue bicycle and pedestrian overpass project over Highway 17
Grant application for bike/pedestrian safety improvements
Feasibility study approval for the overcrossing

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their views on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law or regulation) and skipped the typical waiting period, planning to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading requirement waived to speed up process

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to finalize their 2026 weed control efforts and establish new rules for how the council operates going forward. They also approved displaying Pride and Juneteenth flags, discussed affordable housing policies, and heard a presentation from the regional transit authority about transportation planning.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program across the city
New official procedures and norms for City Council meetings
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
Regional transit and housing implementation plans for the year ahead

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met to review the city's finances for January 2026 and check on progress toward housing and community goals. They approved final plans for a new residential development called Solana Project and set priorities for health and senior services programs for the coming year.
keyTopics: January 2026 financial and investment report
Housing and General Plan progress update
Solana Project residential development approval
2026 health and senior services work plan
Updates to how council meetings are documented

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to organize upcoming council business. They reviewed and made changes to two upcoming agendas—one scheduled for April 7 and another for April 14—by adding new items, scheduling specific times for discussions, and handling any necessary paperwork requirements. The council also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: April 7, 2026 final agenda adjustments and new items
April 14, 2026 draft agenda planning
Schedule assignments for agenda item discussions
Public records review from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed committee reports and scheduled upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling for boards and commissions meetings
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review and approve funding applications for community development programs that help local residents with housing and social services. The commission heard presentations from organizations seeking grants to support these efforts and approved meeting minutes from their previous session.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications from local organizations
Housing and homelessness programs seeking city support
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed forgiving unpaid business licenses for companies that sign up for an amnesty program, promoting local shopping through a summer campaign, and offering financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - giving companies a chance to catch up on unpaid licenses
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to encourage residents to buy from Cupertino businesses
New incentive programs to attract retail stores and startups to the city
Public-private partnerships to fund community events and celebrations
Outreach efforts to shopping centers to boost local business participation

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026 to discuss buildings or sites of historical importance in the city. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom, and had the option to submit written comments by email before the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission via email by noon on meeting day
Review of Santa Clara's historical properties or landmarks

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative housekeeping items like approving previous meeting minutes and setting the calendar for upcoming meetings. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to a 2026 wellness conference and confirmed a new representative to serve on the Economic Development committee from the school district.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming meeting dates
Authorized Mayor Montano to attend WELL 2026 Annual Conference
Appointed new Milpitas Unified School District representative to Economic Development committee
Reviewed councilmember requests for future agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several items including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. The council also authorized the city to partner with the American Red Cross to use city facilities as emergency shelters during disasters, and approved a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software services costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency disaster shelters partnership with Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
